input.background: Ten people are given a choice of two treatments. Let $p$ represent the proportion of patients in the patient population who prefer treatment A. Among the 10 patients asked, 7 preferred method A. Assuming there is no preference in the patient population (i.e., $p= 0.5)$.
input.question: Calculate $\operatorname{Pr}(X \geq 7)$, assuming $X \sim \mathrm{~b}(10,0.5)$.

meta_info.caption: Patient preference.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
